sourceMovie.title: Oz the Great and Powerful
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Oz the Great and Powerful (2013) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Sam Raimi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wizard of Oz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Oz the Great and Powerful clicked for you, The Wizard of Oz (1939) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Oz the Great and Powerful?
faq.answer: Try The Wizard of Oz, Stardust, Inkheart—each shares DNA with Oz the Great and Powerful while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Oz the Great and Powerful?
faq.answer: Oz the Great and Powerful (2013) is best described as Fantasy, Adventure, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Oz the Great and Powerful worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
